channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel, used by millions of websites
item.description: Web hosts are scrambling to fix the bug under active attack by hackers. One company said hackers have been abusing the bug for months.

item.title: Has smart home tech gone too far? This robot vacuum is also a closet — and four washer-dryers
item.description: This Dreame robot vacuum comes with four washer dryers — and a closet

item.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
item.description: OpenAI will begin rolling out it cybersecurity testing tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber only "to critical cyber defenders" at first.

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox mode is now available for all Windows 11 PCs
item.description: Microsoft is now rolling out its Xbox mode to all Windows 11 PCs. The new Xbox mode adds a full-screen interface to the Xbox PC app, much like Steam's Big Picture Mode, and originally debuted as the Xbox Full Screen Experience (FSE) with Asus' Xbox Ally devices. "Some players in select markets will be able […]

item.title: Meta threatens to pull its apps from New Mexico if forced to make ‘technologically impractical’ changes
item.description: Meta says it may be forced to pull Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp from New Mexico if the attorney general gets his way. The state is demanding a host of changes that the company says are impossible to achieve. After winning a $375 million jury award against Meta in a trial that argued the company misled […]

item.title: The minimalist Light Phone III will soon support a curated set of third-party apps
item.description: An SDK will allow developers to start building non-commercial tools soon.

item.title: With Saros, Housemarque makes a case for doing next-gen games differently
item.description: It is generally frowned upon to care too much about appearances. We have a lot of little aphorisms discouraging this - books and their covers, beauty being skin deep, style over substance, that sort of thing. Vanity is a risk. Should one put a disproportionate effort into how a thing looks, then said work may […]

item.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents are circulating from the earliest days of OpenAI - and from before the AI lab even had a name. Some high-level takeaways: […]

item.title: EV startup Faraday Future paid $7.5M to company tied to founder Jia Yueting
item.description: The perpetually struggling EV company made the payments while being investigated by the SEC. That four-year probe was ultimately closed in March.

item.title: Musk v. Altman Kicks Off, DOJ Guts Voting Rights Unit, and Is the AI Job Apocalypse Overhyped?
item.description: In this episode of Uncanny Valley, we get into how the Elon Musk-Sam Altman trial goes way beyond their rivalry and could have major implications both for OpenAI and also the AI industry at large.

item.title: City of None is the next game from the co-creator of Celeste
item.description: Extremely OK Games is publishing the project.

item.title: 'PIN and data remain encrypted at rest': This unhackable offline device is quietly solving a problem most companies don’t realize is about to cost them everything
item.description: Apricorn’s 32TB encrypted drive removes the host computer from security processes, reducing software-based attack exposure while introducing new physical access considerations.

item.title: AI can reason like a doctor, study says
item.description: The results are promising, but they don't mean doctors can be replaced by AI.

item.title: Anbernic's swiveling retro handheld will be available May 11
item.description: The RG Rotate starts at just $88.

item.title: One tool call to rule them all? New open source Python tool RunPod Flash eliminates containers for faster AI dev
item.description: Runpod, the high-performance cloud computing and GPU platform designed specifically for AI development, today launched a new open source, MIT licensed, enterprise-friendly Python programming tool called Runpod Flash — and it is poised to make creation, iteration and deployment of AI systems inside and outside of foundation model labs much faster. The tool aims to eliminate some of the biggest barriers and hurdles to training and using AI models today, namely, doing away with Docker packages and containerization when developing for serverless GPU infrastructure, which the company believes will ...

item.title: LG's UltraGear evo 5K gaming monitor is available to pre-order
item.description: The monitor uses a Mini LED screen and supports AI upscaling for lower resolution content.

item.title: OpenAI announces new advanced security for ChatGPT accounts, including a partnership with Yubico
item.description: OpenAI is launching additional opt-in protections for ChatGPT accounts. The new security initiative includes a new partnership with security key provider Yubico.

item.title: MAGA Is Confused About ‘Animal Farm’
item.description: The new film is the latest adaptation of George Orwell’s novel. Based on the critiques coming from right-wing influencers, it seems few of them fully grasp the book’s message.

item.title: AI rollouts fail because of culture
item.description: Companies are pouring money into AI, but without changing how work gets done, it won’t pay off. Ask any C-suite leader if AI is a priority in their organization. The answer is yes.

item.title: Elon Musk confirms xAI used OpenAI’s models to train Grok
item.description: In a federal courtroom in California on Thursday, Elon Musk testified that his own AI startup, xAI, has used OpenAI's models to improve its own. The matter at question is model distillation, a common industry practice by which one larger AI model acts as a "teacher" of sorts to pass on knowledge to a smaller […]

item.title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
item.description: "Distillation" is a hot topic as frontier labs try to prevent smaller competitors from copying their models.

item.title: Révélation : les mystérieux « points rouges » découverts par le télescope James-Webb seraient bien des étoiles à trou noir !
item.description: Les fameux Little Red Dots (LRD) ou « petits points rouges » en français, qui ont été découverts par le télescope James-Webb, ont donné lieu à plusieurs explications possibles quant à leur vraie nature. Un objet similaire, mais moins ancien, avait en fait été détecté en rayons X par le satellite...

item.title: While the Apple Watch needs charging every night, Suunto could be prepping a smartwatch with battery life measured in years — to take on the Garmin Fenix
item.description: A new FCC filing suggests that we're about to see the launch of the Suunto Core 2, with a replaceable battery.

item.title: What is the release date for Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 10 on CBS and Paramount+?
item.description: Last week, Kayce risked everything to save one of his own. But when does Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 10 hit CBS and Paramount+?

item.title: Is Trump NACHO the next Trump TACO? Why stock market trading terms sound like a menu
item.description: The internet is picking up a new acronym to denigrate President Trump’s actions, and it’s also food-themed. First TACO, and now NACHO: The acronyms created for a snappy way to denigrate Trump’s actions are starting to sound like a menu.

item.title: FDA approval, fundraising, and the reality of building in healthcare according to BioticsAI founder
item.description: BioticsAI CEO Robhy Bustami joined Isabelle Johannessen on Build Mode to discuss how the company has navigated a highly regulated space and kept the team motivated while cutting through all the red tape.

item.title: Xbox owners can now disable Quick Resume for specific games
item.description: Microsoft has released a new Xbox update that adds a bunch of new features, including the ability to disable Quick Resume for individual games. Quick Resume, which lets you swap between your games with minimal wait time, is one of the best features on the Xbox Series X / S consoles. But it can also […]

item.title: Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 support for PlayStation VR2 is now live
item.description: Asobo Studio says it also improved the game's performance with the latest update.

item.title: Google’s Gemini AI assistant is hitting the road in millions of vehicles
item.description: The move signals Google’s push to bring more advanced, conversational AI into the driving experience.

item.title: Senate Judiciary Committee unanimously approves AI chatbot age verification
item.description: It's a rare moment of working across the aisle.

item.title: Don’t Let Industry Jargon Cost You When Shopping for a Smart Bed
item.description: Why the terms “smart bed,” "adjustable mattress," and “adjustable frame” are not interchangeable, and how to avoid an expensive mistake.

item.title: Elon Musk Seemingly Admits xAI Has Used OpenAI’s Models to Train Its Own
item.description: While answering questions under oath, Musk argued it’s standard practice for AI labs to use their competitors’ models.
